metrics.narrative: BMI engaged deeply with quantified value during the 1 Dec 2025 call: sourcing-savings benchmarks of 0.6 to 1.5% of addressable spend and 20 to 40% FTE efficiency gains were discussed, alongside high-level cost figures (USD 220K per year subscription plus USD 380 to 400K one-time implementation). Venkat pressed hard on the specifics ('Can I expect a 30% efficiency gain, or would it stay something between 20%?'), showing real scrutiny of the business case rather than passive interest.

sources: Avoma call notes, 1 Dec 2025 and 17 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Venkata Masetty has been the constant, responsive driver across every touchpoint from 1 Dec 2025 through 19 Jun 2026: he pressed on ROI specifics, coordinated the RFP, and said in Dec 2025 he was 'preparing an internal business case to present to management for budget approval.' He is a developing champion, not yet proven: there is no evidence he has secured access to an economic buyer or advocated for Zycus in a room without a Zycus rep present.

competition.narrative: No competing vendor has been named by BMI in any call. Coupa came up once, on 17 Jun 2026, but only as Caroline's former employer, explicitly noted in the meeting record as 'context only; no competitor vendors evaluated by BMI stated in meeting.' BMI's RFI/RFP framing implies other supplier-platform vendors may be in the mix, but none is confirmed.

identify_pain.narrative: BMI's pain is specific and repeatedly corroborated: a fragmented supplier environment across roughly 40,000 supplier records spread over SAP and non-SAP ERPs, duplicate supplier records, inconsistent onboarding, weak risk and ESG visibility, and multilingual/regional gaps (e.g. French, Bulgarian). These were named directly by BMI's team on 17 Jun 2026 ('Prevent and resolve duplicate supplier records during onboarding and via mass migration from multiple ERPs').

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, order form, or legal contact has entered the conversation at any point in the record. The deal is still in Formal Evaluation with commercials only recently shared (3 Jun 2026); paper process has not started.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has appeared on any call across eleven months of engagement. The CRM MEDDPICC record (updated 20 Mar 2026) states the EB is 'not yet clearly identified.' Sharon Moar (Group Category Director) and Jeremy Dussel (Procurement Director) attended the 17 Jun 2026 demo, but neither has been confirmed as the final budget approver or executive sponsor, and no CFO, CPO, or C-level contact has joined a call.

decision_process.narrative: A process is visible in the timeline (RFP Q&A call 6 May 2026, RFP submitted 8 May 2026, commercials follow-up call 28 May 2026, two pricing options sent 3 Jun 2026, demo 17 Jun 2026), but the internal approval chain and a named decision date beyond the placeholder close of 15 Sep 2026 have not been confirmed. The CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 field is 'N.A.' on this element as of 20 Mar 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: BMI's criteria are well documented from its RFP process: a proven off-the-shelf SaaS platform with a unified interface spanning supplier vetting and onboarding, communication, performance management, risk and compliance, contract and document management, reporting and analytics, and e-sourcing, plus scalability and ERP/third-party data integration. The 17 Jun 2026 demo confirmed BMI is testing this criteria module by module (iSupplier, iRisk, iContract, iSource).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: The deal shows genuinely deep, tailored engagement and a well-documented pain and requirements set, but sits capped by a real access-to-power gap: no economic buyer has engaged in eleven months, budget approval status is unconfirmed since December 2025, and a live AI-data-governance concern is still open with only a verbal answer.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on Venkata Masetty for real advocacy: Sharon Moar, Jeremy Dussel, Julia Alenina and Dorothee attended the 17 Jun 2026 demo but none has independently engaged since.
The AI and data-training governance concern raised 17 Jun 2026 is unresolved beyond a verbal assurance and could stall contracting once the deal reaches that stage.
The 15 Sep 2026 close date leaves 68 days for vendor selection, EB engagement, commercial agreement, and full contracting, none of which have meaningfully started.

win_position.text: Deep, tailored engagement continues: Sharon Moar called the 17 Jun 2026 demo well received and asked for further follow-up sessions, with detailed module-level questions from the whole BMI team.
Decision criteria are well documented: BMI's RFP (submitted 8 May 2026) names a unified SaaS platform spanning onboarding, risk, contracts, sourcing and analytics, matching Zycus's suite pitch.

No economic buyer has appeared on any call in eleven months: Sharon Moar (Group Category Director) and Jeremy Dussel (Procurement Director) attended the 17 Jun demo but neither is confirmed as the final budget approver.
Budget approval status is unconfirmed since Venkata said in Dec 2025 a decision was expected 'before year-end'; no update has followed through 9 Jul 2026.
BMI's AI-data-governance requirement (written proof Zycus won't train models on their data) is still open with only a verbal answer given on 17 Jun 2026, and could block the paper process.

deal_momentum.text: A real two-way buyer touch landed as recently as 19 Jun 2026 (Venkat's follow-up items after the demo), and Caroline's 9 Jul 2026 outreach for an updated timeline keeps the thread active.
Commercial topics have entered the conversation: two pricing options (Core, and Core plus Optional Modules) were sent to BMI on 3 Jun 2026.
No date has been set for a decision or vendor selection since the RFP was submitted 8 May 2026; Caroline's 9 Jul 2026 ask for an updated timeline and next steps is still awaiting a reply.
Sharon Moar did not answer four outbound calls between 22 May and 5 Jun 2026 before re-engaging for the 17 Jun demo, showing patchy responsiveness outside of scheduled sessions.

customer_commitment.text: Venkata Masetty has stayed the consistent, responsive driver across every touchpoint from 1 Dec 2025 through 19 Jun 2026, coordinating internal requirements and business-case preparation.
The internal business case Venkata said he would present to management for budget approval on 1 Dec 2025 has no confirmed outcome eight months later.
